mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when PAYEMS growth remains positive and above historical median.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133019.0 thousand as of September 2014 indicates continued labor market recovery
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and forward returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows no acceleration above 2.5% year-over-year, the real policy rate is deeply negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity risk appetite. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates only when both conditions hold simultaneously, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% and CPIAUCSL at 212.714 as of September 2014
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with contained inflation
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) move together as joint indicators of consumer and construction demand. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this signals broad-based demand expansion that supports equity valuations. The interaction between housing and retail demand is non-linear: both must strengthen together to trigger the mechanism, predicting positive returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 0% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 583.0 thousand and RSAFS at 344380.0 million as of August 2014
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS show positive month-over-month growth, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10), or interaction Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential demand destruction, which compresses profit margins and reduces equity valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when INDPRO shows contraction, as supply weakness typically precedes broader economic slowdown.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month declines fail to produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and forward returns exceeds -0.05 (i.e., signal is too weak or reversed).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 8.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which depresses equity valuations through lower expected earnings and reduced risk appetite. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates when UNRATE crosses above 8.0%, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.5% as of September 2014 indicates elevated unemployment
regime_hint: labor market stress or post-shock recovery phase
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 8.0%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE level and forward returns is less negative than -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows the fundamental demand trajectory. When GDPC1 growth accelerates quarter-over-quarter above 2.5% annualized rate, this signals robust economic expansion that supports equity valuations through higher corporate earnings. This regime-conditional mechanism is most powerful in late-cycle expansion phases and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11522.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2014
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive (mean < 1.0% or t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.07 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% creates negative real rates that erode bond returns and force equity risk premium compression. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both conditions to activate simultaneously, predicting positive equity returns over 5 trading days as investors flee bonds into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% and CPIAUCSL at 212.714 as of September 2014
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) move together as joint indicators of productive capacity utilization and labor demand. When both decline simultaneously month-over-month, this signals synchronized supply and labor weakness that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures the non-linear risk of coordinated economic contraction that single-series signals miss.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.3755 and PAYEMS at 133019.0 as of September 2014
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor contraction indicating recession risk
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline month-over-month, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative (mean > -0.5% or t-test p > 0.10), or interaction Rank IC > -0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
